subject: [PATCH v3] mm: migrate: requeue destination folio on deferred split queue

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the destination THPs were being removed from the deferred split queue during migration, and explained that this patch adds them back to the list after migration to meet user expectations.
reviews.raw_body:
The original patches added THPs to deferred_list at fault/collapse, they
got removed but not added back to the list after migration.
This patch adds them to the deferred_list on migration. The user would
not expect the THPs to get removed from deferred_list on migration, so
this fixes user expectations.

I have CC-ed stable@vger.kernel.org to this email. Should I resend the patch
with CC stable in commit message?

2026-03-13.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the Linux kernel's folio migration code by re-queuing the destination folio on the deferred split queue after a successful migration. The source folio is removed from the queue, but the destination folio remains and can cause underutilized THPs to escape the shrinker. The patch records whether the source folio was on the deferred split queue and its partially mapped state before unqueuing it, and re-queues the destination folio if necessary. This ensures that fully mapped but potentially underused folios are correctly handled.

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton questioned whether the patch provides a tangible benefit to userspace, suggesting that if no benefits can be identified, it may not meet the criteria for backporting.
reviews.raw_body:
Maybe users just won't notice?

If we can't identify any benefit to userspace then I don't think this
patch meets the criteria for backporting.

reviews.author: SeongJae Park
reviews.summary: Reviewer SeongJae Park noted that the patch's fix may lead to underutilized THPs being split by the shrinker, resulting in users seeing less free memory than before, and requested Usama to correct his understanding.
reviews.raw_body:
My guess of the user-visible consequence was like following.  Because THPs are
removed from the deferred_list, THP shinker cannot split the underutilized THPs
in time.  As a result, users will show less free memory than before.  I believe
I might be wrong and Usama can correct me in the case.


Thanks,
SJ

[...]

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton approved the patch, stating that it will be included in the next stable release and does not need to be rushed into mainline.
reviews.raw_body:
That'll do, thanks ;)

Pasted, added cc:stable.  It's been there since 6.12 so I don't see a
need to rush this in, so I won't move this into mm-hotfixes - it'll go
into mainline for 7.1-rc1 after which -stable should pick it up.

2026-03-14.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the Linux kernel's folio migration code by re-queuing the destination folio on the deferred split queue after a successful migration. The source folio is removed from the queue, but the destination folio remains and can cause underutilized THPs to escape the shrinker. The patch records whether the source folio was on the deferred split queue and its partially mapped state before unqueuing it, and re-queues the destination folio if necessary. This ensures that fully mapped but potentially underused folios are correctly handled.
